document_metadata.title: IPL 2023: Venkatesh Iyer ends long wait for a KKR century since Brendom McCullum’s in first IPL game
document_metadata.first_words: On 18 April 2008, Brendon McCullum gave a dream start to the Indian Premier League with a stunning 158* for Kolkata Knight Riders against Royal Challengers Bangalore at the M Chinnaswamy stadium in the first ever match of the tournament. On 16 April 2023, Venkatesh Iyer ended a long wait for a second century by a Kolkata Knight Riders batter, scoring a century against Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium. A total of 5,476 days have passed between the two centuries. McCullum’s 158 is still considered one of the best knocks in the history of the tournament

content_metadata.document_title_txt: IPL 2023: Venkatesh Iyer ends long wait for a KKR century since Brendom McCullum’s in first IPL game

chunks.content_original:
> Pretty historic day   
>   
> Tendulkar’s are the first father-son pair to play in IPL.  
>   
> Marco Jansen and Duan Jansen are the first twins to play in IPL.  
>   
> Venkatesh Iyer only 2nd KKR batsman to get a 100 after Brendon McCullum![#MIvKKR](https://twitter.com/hashtag/MIvKKR?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) [#IPL23](https://twitter.com/hashtag/IPL23?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw)
>
> — Saiyami Kher (@SaiyamiKher) [April 16, 2023](https://twitter.com/SaiyamiKher/status/1647565502036754433?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw)
> 2 - Only two players have ever scored a ton for [@KKRiders](https://twitter.com/KKRiders?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) in the [@IPL](https://twitter.com/IPL?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) - [@venkateshiyer](https://twitter.com/venkateshiyer?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) (104) in the [#MIvKKR](https://twitter.com/hashtag/MIvKKR?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) game today and [@Bazmccullum](https://twitter.com/Bazmccullum?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) (158\* vs RCB) in the opening game (18 April 2008) of the competition; this is also Iyer's maiden ton in the IPL. Bravado. [#TATAIPL](https://twitter.com/hashtag/TATAIPL?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw)

chunks.content_original:
![](https://sc0.blr1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/article/189170-klgindakyy-1681646491.jpg)

On 18 April 2008, Brendon McCullum gave a dream start to the Indian Premier League with a stunning 158\* for Kolkata Knight Riders against Royal Challengers Bangalore at the M Chinnaswamy stadium in the first ever match of the tournament.

On 16 April 2023, Venkatesh Iyer ended a long wait for a second century by a Kolkata Knight Riders batter, scoring a century against Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium.

A total of 5,476 days have passed between the two centuries.

McCullum’s 158 is still considered one of the best knocks in the history of the tournament as he pulverised the RCB bowling attack that day in Bengaluru. Since then, a total of seven scores in 90s were scored by KKR batters, with Dinesh Karthik coming closest with a 97\* in April 2019.

chunks.content_original:
On Sunday though, the wait was ended by Venkatesh Iyer’s 49-ball century. Having come in to bat at No 3, at the 11-over mark Iyer had scored nearly 75% off KKR’s runs, and hit 100% of the boundaries KKR had scored till then. He was hit on his knee earlier in the innings and had trouble running between the wickets, but stood upright and delivered some massive blows.

He finished with a 51-ball 104 in the end, in an innings with six fours and nine sixes.

Iyer’s knock was the second century in IPL 2023, following Harry Brook’s against KKR at Eden Gardens. It was, however, not enough for KKR as MI coasted to a win thanks to Ishan Kishan and Suryakumar Yadav’s knocks.

*[IPL 2023: Ishan Kishan stars as MI defeat KKR, Venkatesh Iyer’s century in vain](https://scroll.in/field/1047459/ipl-2023-ishan-kishan-stars-as-mi-defeat-kkr-venkatesh-iyers-century-in-vain)*
> [#IPL](https://twitter.com/hashtag/IPL?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) 100s for [#KKR](https://twitter.com/hashtag/KKR?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw)   
> 158\* - Brendon McCullum in the inaugural match in 2008 on 18 April 2008 - 100 in 53 balls  
> 15 years later!

Mon, 17 Apr 2023 05:51:51 GMT https://scroll.in/field/1047453/ipl-2023-venkatesh-iyer-ends-long-wait-for-a-kkr-century-since-brendom-mccullums-in-first-ipl-game?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=prorata Scroll Staff 2023-04-17T05:51:51Z

On 18 April 2008, Brendon McCullum gave a dream start to the Indian Premier League with a stunning 158* for Kolkata Knight Riders against Royal Challengers Bangalore at the M Chinnaswamy stadium in the first ever match of the tournament.

On 16 April 2023, Venkatesh Iyer ended a long wait for a second century by a Kolkata Knight Riders batter, scoring a century against Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium.

A total of 5,476 days have passed between the two centuries.

McCullum’s 158 is still considered one of the best knocks in the history of the tournament as he pulverised the RCB bowling attack that day in Bengaluru. Since then, a total of seven scores in 90s were scored by KKR batters, with Dinesh Karthik coming closest with a 97* in April 2019.

On Sunday though, the wait was ended by Venkatesh Iyer’s 49-ball century. Having come in to bat at No 3, at the 11-over mark Iyer had scored nearly 75% off KKR’s runs, and hit 100% of the boundaries KKR had scored till then. He was hit on his knee earlier in the innings and had trouble running between the wickets, but stood upright and delivered some massive blows.

He finished with a 51-ball 104 in the end, in an innings with six fours and nine sixes.

Iyer’s knock was the second century in IPL 2023, following Harry Brook’s against KKR at Eden Gardens. It was, however, not enough for KKR as MI coasted to a win thanks to Ishan Kishan and Suryakumar Yadav’s knocks.

IPL 2023: Ishan Kishan stars as MI defeat KKR, Venkatesh Iyer’s century in vain

#IPL 100s for #KKR
158* - Brendon McCullum in the inaugural match in 2008 on 18 April 2008 - 100 in 53 balls
15 years later!
100* - Venkatesh Iyer TODAY! on 16 Apr 2023 - 100 in 49 balls!#IPL2023#KKRvMI #MIvKKR#KKRvsMI #MIvsKKR

— Mohandas Menon (@mohanstatsman) April 16, 2023

Highest scores by KKR batters in IPL

Player Runs Balls SR Opposition Ground Match Date
BB McCullum 158* 73 216.43 v RCB Bengaluru 18 Apr 2008
VR Iyer 104 51 203.92 v Mumbai Wankhede 16 Apr 2023
KD Karthik 97* 50 194.00 v Royals Eden Gardens 25 Apr 2019

markdown_content:
![](https://sc0.blr1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/article/189170-klgindakyy-1681646491.jpg)

On 18 April 2008, Brendon McCullum gave a dream start to the Indian Premier League with a stunning 158\* for Kolkata Knight Riders against Royal Challengers Bangalore at the M Chinnaswamy stadium in the first ever match of the tournament.

On 16 April 2023, Venkatesh Iyer ended a long wait for a second century by a Kolkata Knight Riders batter, scoring a century against Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium.

A total of 5,476 days have passed between the two centuries.

McCullum’s 158 is still considered one of the best knocks in the history of the tournament as he pulverised the RCB bowling attack that day in Bengaluru. Since then, a total of seven scores in 90s were scored by KKR batters, with Dinesh Karthik coming closest with a 97\* in April 2019.

On Sunday though, the wait was ended by Venkatesh Iyer’s 49-ball century. Having come in to bat at No 3, at the 11-over mark Iyer had scored nearly 75% off KKR’s runs, and hit 100% of the boundaries KKR had scored till then. He was hit on his knee earlier in the innings and had trouble running between the wickets, but stood upright and delivered some massive blows.

He finished with a 51-ball 104 in the end, in an innings with six fours and nine sixes.

Iyer’s knock was the second century in IPL 2023, following Harry Brook’s against KKR at Eden Gardens. It was, however, not enough for KKR as MI coasted to a win thanks to Ishan Kishan and Suryakumar Yadav’s knocks.

*[IPL 2023: Ishan Kishan stars as MI defeat KKR, Venkatesh Iyer’s century in vain](https://scroll.in/field/1047459/ipl-2023-ishan-kishan-stars-as-mi-defeat-kkr-venkatesh-iyers-century-in-vain)*

> [#IPL](https://twitter.com/hashtag/IPL?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) 100s for [#KKR](https://twitter.com/hashtag/KKR?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw)   
> 158\* - Brendon McCullum in the inaugural match in 2008 on 18 April 2008 - 100 in 53 balls  
> 15 years later!  
> 100\* - Venkatesh Iyer TODAY! on 16 Apr 2023 - 100 in 49 balls![#IPL2023](https://twitter.com/hashtag/IPL2023?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw)[#KKRvMI](https://twitter.com/hashtag/KKRvMI?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) [#MIvKKR](https://twitter.com/hashtag/MIvKKR?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw)[#KKRvsMI](https://twitter.com/hashtag/KKRvsMI?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) [#MIvsKKR](https://twitter.com/hashtag/MIvsKKR?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw)
>
> — Mohandas Menon (@mohanstatsman) [April 16, 2023](https://twitter.com/mohanstatsman/status/1647563876706430981?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw)

| KD Karthik | 97\* | 50 | 194.00 | v Royals | Eden Gardens | 25 Apr 2019 |
| MK Pandey | 94 | 50 | 188.00 | v Kings XI | Bengaluru | 1 Jun 2014 |

| G Gambhir | 93 | 51 | 182.35 | v RCB | Eden Gardens | 28 Apr 2012 |
| MS Bisla | 92 | 61 | 150.81 | v Super Kings | Chennai | 28 Apr 2013 |
| SC Ganguly | 91 | 57 | 159.64 | v Chargers | Hyderabad | 11 May 2008 |
